title: Understanding Matrix Multiplication
summary: Matrix multiplication is a binary operation that takes two matrices and produces another matrix. This process involves the dot product of rows from the first matrix with columns in the second, assuming compatible dimensions.

sections.heading: What it is
sections.body: Matrix multiplication combines two matrices to form a new one by performing dot products between corresponding rows and columns. Unlike regular arithmetic addition or subtraction, which are commutative but not associative in the case of non-square matrices.
key_points: Requires compatible dimensions for both matrices
Resulting matrix size depends on smaller dimension's values

sections.heading: How it works
sections.body: To multiply two matrices, each element of the resulting matrix is calculated by taking the dot product between a row from one matrix and a column from the other. This involves multiplying corresponding elements together and summing these products.
key_points: Sum up all products for an element

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.body: Matrix multiplication is fundamental in various fields, such as computer graphics, engineering, and economics. It helps to model complex systems by transforming data into new formats that reveal underlying patterns.
